key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $793.909M ÷ 341.023566M shares; total revenue $3224.412M and provision for credit losses $616.683M (AFRM/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250828_rpt20250630_acc0001820953_25_000080.htm)
Continued GMV growth with stable credit losses; OCF normalized off FY2025

open_questions: Confirm live price before final base IRR after mechanical refresh.
Wallet/card option band (**$0–$22/sh**) requires human judgment before decision-grade use.
Promote any approved third-party source before blending into base IRR.
thesis: Affirm Holdings operates a buy-now-pay-later and consumer installment lending platform integrated at merchant checkout and through its mobile app. **[Fact]** Shares trade on Nasdaq (AFRM). **[Fact]** FY2025 total revenue was **$3.22 billion**, operating income was **$1.20 billion**, and net income was **$985.3 million** per `investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20250828_rpt20250630_acc0001820953_25_000080.htm`. **[Fact]** FY2025 operating cash flow was **$793.9 million** on **341.0 million** weighted-average diluted shares (**$2.33** per share). **[Fact]** At March 31, 2026, cash was **$1.72 billion** and total long-term debt was **$8.87 billion**, mostly securitization funding matched to loan receivables, per `investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260507_rpt20260331_acc0001628280_26_032294.htm`.
why_market_wrong: The stock may embed sustained hyper-growth and benign credit while filing-locked owner cash already supports a mid-teens component value far below price. **[Inference]** Normalized operating cash flow of **$2.33 per share** capitalized at a conservative nine-times multiple yields about **$21 per share** before options and reserves, versus a price near **$75**, so the market prices years of above-trend GMV and wallet expansion not yet in base owner cash. **[Inference]** Parent liquidity of roughly **$1.71 per share** (cash less corporate debt, excluding securitization) limits near-term balance-sheet distress but does not justify the full premium. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to about **$18.67 per share**, implying the market assigns substantial value to Affirm Card and wallet optionality and to credit performance better than the reserved stress case.
